name: Robb's Pit-Style Pork Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. At least one hour before grilling, soak wood chips in enough water to cover. Drain the wood chips.
2. Prepare the mustard sauce as directed below.
3. Arrange preheated coals around a drip pan in a covered grill. Test for low heat above the pan.
4. Sprinkle 4 cups of the soaked wood chips over the coals.
5. Place the meat on the grill rack, but not over the coals.
6. Grill, covered, for 3 hours or until well-done (170°F) or until the internal temperature reaches 170°F. Allow 35 to 40 minutes per pound.
7. Adjust vents and add more charcoal and wood chips as necessary. Brush often with sauce during the last hour.
8. Prepare the sauce by combining all ingredients in a 1-quart saucepan.
9. Over high heat, bring the sauce to boiling.
10. Reduce heat to low; simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes, stirring often.
11. Let stand at least 1 hour before serving.
